findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 34. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The main map image in the HTML is extremely small and lacks legibility compared to the PDF. The PDF shows a detailed, full-page map with clearly readable labels, sediment data callouts, road names, property boundaries, and legend. The HTML renders this as a tiny thumbnail that loses all detail and legibility.
findings.evidence: PDF: Full-page detailed map showing stream sediment assessment with readable station labels (SW/SD-1 through SW/SD-5), sediment data tables, road names, property boundaries, scale bar, and legend. HTML: Same map rendered as a small thumbnail image where none of the details, labels, or data callouts are legible.

findings.description: The PDF presents sediment data results as spatial callout boxes positioned on the map at their respective sampling locations, providing geographic context. The HTML converts these into a sequential list of flat tables, losing the spatial relationship between the data and the map locations.
findings.evidence: PDF: SD-1 through SD-5 data boxes are positioned at specific locations on the map, each connected to its sampling point. HTML: All sediment data is presented as a linear sequence of labeled sections (SD-1/DUP-6102025-SD, SD-2, SD-3, SD-4, SD-5) without spatial context.

findings.description: The sediment data in the PDF is presented in compact labeled tables within map callout boxes with clear column headers (parameter and mg/kg). The HTML renders these as two-column tables but some lack explicit column headers, and the Risk Assessment sub-sections are formatted inconsistently compared to the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: Each station box has a clear header line (e.g., 'SD-2  mg/kg') followed by parameter-value pairs, then 'Risk Assessment' with CR and HI values. HTML: Similar data is present but structured as separate table rows with 'Risk Assessment' appearing as a text row rather than a sub-header, and column headers vary (some show 'mg/kg' in the header row, others show 'Risk Assessment' as a value).

findings.description: Risk Assessment rows are embedded within the data tables as regular data rows rather than being a separate sub-section. The 'Risk Assessment' label appears as both columns (e.g., 'Risk Assessment | Risk Assessment') which is not how it appears in the PDF where it is a sub-header within each callout box.
findings.evidence: In each HTML table, there is a row 'Risk AssessmentRisk Assessment' which duplicates the label across both columns. In the PDF, 'Risk Assessment' is an underlined sub-header label, not a two-column data row.

findings.description: The REFERENCE section is rendered as an h4 under NOTES which is an h3, but in the PDF 'REFERENCE:' is at the same level as the notes header - both are bold labels within the notes block. This is a minor hierarchy interpretation difference.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows 'NOTES:' and 'REFERENCE:' as same-level bold labels. HTML has '

NOTES:

' and '

REFERENCE:

' creating a parent-child relationship not present in the PDF.
